codeReview.summary: The fix directly addresses the prior blocking failure: hydration now normalizes legacy duplicate rows inside fetchActiveProfileState using an ordered canonical-row selection before returning state, and new regression coverage exercises legacy multi-row fixtures plus repeated relaunch reads. Compared with the original failed commit (6ae375d), this closes the read-path gap that previously left unordered LIMIT 1 behavior during relaunch hydration.

issues.description: Canonical selection orders only by updatedAt DESC when choosing the survivor row during cleanup. If legacy duplicate rows share identical updatedAt values, survivor choice can still be tie-dependent (no explicit secondary tiebreaker such as rowid/id), so strict determinism is not fully encoded for timestamp ties.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked followedProcedure=true, but transcript evidence does not show reading .factory/library/user-testing.md even though switcher-core-worker requires it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill requirement: .factory/skills/switcher-core-worker/SKILL.md (Work Procedure step 1) explicitly requires reading mission.md, AGENTS.md, .factory/library/architecture.md, and .factory/library/user-testing.md. Transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 804fe1c7-e070-4df3-a70a-26deb9e46b58 shows Read calls for mission.md, validation-contract.md, AGENTS.md, services.yaml, and architecture.md, but no read of user-testing.md.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill discipline requires explicit context-read evidence in handoff verification when followedProcedure=true, but this handoff only lists test commands.

summary: Pass. The fix commit (b20039bb8009e3d6c0bd8f64553587ca003dbb41) remedies the prior failure from core-engine-fix-active-profile-determinism by adding deterministic legacy-row cleanup during hydration and adding targeted regression tests for multi-row relaunch scenarios. One non-blocking edge case remains around equal-timestamp tie handling in canonical-row selection.
